summary: Iteration 3 — Full validation of (a) /api/share-excerpt new styles 'Prayer' (first-person preserving) and 'Verse', (b) ShareImageModal new 3-button row (Copy/Save/Share), (c) Devotional share button on Scripture tab using Claude excerpt, (d) dedicated PrayerShareCard with 4 mood templates on Prayer tab, (e) no regression on previously passing endpoints. Backend: 25/25 pytest tests PASS — added test_prayer_excerpt_preserves_first_person (5-line prayer → 280-char excerpt that retains 'I'/'my'/'me' tokens) and test_verse_excerpt (NLT-style verse text). All existing tests (daily-verse NLT bible/116/ link + local_date determinism, prayer-request VERSE: marker, prayer-follow-up implicit through theological flow, react-to-verse pray/love/fire/insight, theological-question Devotional|Theologian + 422 on Pastoral, share-excerpt Devotional caching + Theologian + short text + empty 400 + invalid style 422, reflections CRUD + 404s + 400 empty) still PASS. Frontend (mobile viewport 390x844): Scripture tab loads with verse-card; new 'share-devotional-button' (small icon next to 'Devotional' label) opens the Share modal showing a DEVOTIONAL REFLECTION card with the Claude-generated excerpt; modal has the new 3-button action row in correct order (Copy left, Save center, Share gold-primary right); tapping Copy shows 'Copied' toast (verified visually — pill above buttons) and the modal stays open (toast auto-dismisses). All three Devotional templates (Editorial/Reflection/Quote) and all three aspects (Post/Square/Story) re-render the preview correctly. Cancel closes the modal. Prayer tab: typed 'I'm anxious about an upcoming meeting at work', tapped Begin → reflection-card appeared (Claude reply with VERSE: line, here Isaiah 41:10), tapped 'Pray with me' → prayer-card appeared (first-person 'A PRAYER FOR YOU' content); tapping share-prayer-button opens the new 'Share your prayer' modal with the dedicated PrayerShareCard preview (NOT QAShareCard) — verified by visible 'A PRAYER' label and Claude-trimmed first-person excerpt 'I trust You more than my anxiety, and I need Your calm in this moment of fear...'. All four prayer templates (Journal/Centered/Editorial/Candlelight) are present and switchable; all three aspects switchable. Backdrop tap closes the prayer modal. Verse reference appears as footnote in the prayer card. No ScrollView layout leak observed. Save action on web correctly delegates to share/download path (verified by code: handleSaveToPhotos → handleShareImage when Platform.OS === 'web'); native permission flow + Settings fallback wired with MediaLibrary.getPermissionsAsync/requestPermissionsAsync/saveToLibraryAsync + Linking.openSettings, and app.json has NSPhotoLibraryAddUsageDescription + WRITE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE + expo-media-library plugin (cannot fully exercise the native permission UI in Playwright web). Deprecated packages detected: expo-audio + react-native-keyboard-controller version pins still mismatched (carried over from iter1/iter2). RN style-prop deprecation 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents' STILL warns from ShareImageModal.tsx lines 318 and 431 — not fixed in iter3 even though iter2 flagged it.

react_native_issues.issue: Carried over from iter2 — still emits 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents'. Two occurrences remain. Reference: /app/frontend/src/components/ShareImageModal.tsx:318 (sheet View) and /app/frontend/src/components/ShareImageModal.tsx:431 (toast Animated.View). Also a third on /app/frontend/src/components/PrayerShareCard.tsx:269 (Candlelight top vignette LinearGradient pointerEvents='none'). Move pointerEvents into style={{ pointerEvents: 'none' }}.
Carried over — Expo doctor still flags expo-audio version mismatch. Reference: /app/frontend/package.json
Carried over — pin to the version Expo SDK expects. Reference: /app/frontend/package.json
Carried over — still emits 'shadow* style props are deprecated. Use boxShadow.' Reference: scripture.tsx, prayer.tsx, reflections.tsx StyleSheet blocks.
Carried over (web only) — 'Animated: useNativeDriver is not supported because the native animated module is missing. Falling back to JS-based animation.' Cosmetic on web preview; harmless on device.

action_items: Move pointerEvents='none' from JSX props into style={{ pointerEvents: 'none' }} on ShareImageModal.tsx:318, ShareImageModal.tsx:431, and PrayerShareCard.tsx:269 to silence the deprecation warning.
(Carried over) Align expo-audio and react-native-keyboard-controller versions to those Expo SDK expects; migrate shadow*/textShadow* StyleSheet props to boxShadow/textShadow.
(Optional polish) On web, the 'Save' button effectively duplicates 'Share' since both trigger a download. Consider hiding Save on web or relabeling to make the duplication intentional.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py share-excerpt for Prayer style: prompt likely instructs Claude to preserve first-person voice. Verified empirically: 5-line prayer 'Heavenly Father, I am tired... Please hold me close... Help me trust You... In Jesus' name, Amen' returns an excerpt that retains first-person markers and stays under 280 chars.

PrayerShareCard architecture is clean: separate component from QAShareCard, four mood templates implemented as inner functions sharing a StyleSheet, format-aware padding/sizing via padFor() and prayerSizing(). Verse reference correctly rendered as a small uppercase letter-spaced footnote rather than a quote graphic — matches the design intent of treating the prayer body as the emotional hero.

ShareImageModal payload discriminated union (kind: 'qa' | 'prayer') is the right abstraction — keeps the modal a single rendered Modal instance with two different inner cards. handleCopyText branches on payload.kind so the clipboard text is appropriately formatted ('A Prayer' header for prayer, '— reference' suffix for qa).

handleSaveToPhotos contextual permission flow is correct: getPermissionsAsync → if undetermined ask, → if denied (canAskAgain=false) show Settings alert. On web it correctly falls back to the share/download path so behavior is consistent.
app.json correctly declares NSPhotoLibraryAddUsageDescription (iOS), WRITE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE (Android), and the expo-media-library plugin — TestFlight/Play builds will have proper permission strings.

Two share buttons on Scripture tab (share-scripture-button for verse + share-devotional-button for devotional) currently both open the SAME ShareImageModal but with different ShareSource payloads — verified by code reading and by observing the modal's preview header changing between 'A DEVOTIONAL REFLECTION' card and the verse-quote card.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on tested critical flows (scripture loads, share-devotional-button opens modal, Copy/Save/Share row present in order with Share gold-primary, Copied toast appears, all 3 Devotional templates + 3 aspects switchable, Cancel closes; prayer flow end-to-end: input → begin → reflection-card → pray-with-me → prayer-card → share-prayer-button opens 'Share your prayer' modal with PrayerShareCard, all 4 prayer templates + 3 aspects switchable, backdrop tap closes; no ScrollView layout leak)

seed_data_creation: Tests create transient reflections prefixed with TEST_ and clean them up. Reactions use unique TST.<uuid>.1 verse_ids. Share-excerpt entries are cached in MongoDB under deterministic SHA-256 keys (intentional persistence — exercises the cache-hit path on re-run, including the new Prayer + Verse styles).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend public base: https://prayers-loft.preview.emergentagent.com. Pytest: `EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL=... pytest /app/backend/tests/test_prayers_loft_api.py -v`. POST /api/share-excerpt now accepts style ∈ {Devotional, Theologian, Prayer, Verse}; Prayer is the only one designed to preserve first-person voice (verified). Frontend testIDs of interest: verse-card, share-scripture-button (verse share), share-devotional-button (NEW — devotional share), share-qa-button (Q&A share), prayer-input, begin-prayer-button, reflection-card, pray-with-me-button, prayer-card, share-prayer-button, save-prayer-button. Share modal titles: 'Share moment' (Scripture devotional + verse + qa), 'Share your prayer' (Prayer tab). Prayer template labels: Journal/Centered/Editorial/Candlelight. Devotional template labels: Editorial/Reflection/Quote. Action button order Copy → Save → Share (Share is the gold primary CTA). Tap 'Copy' shows a 'Copied' toast pill above the buttons (auto-dismiss; modal stays open). On web Save and Share both trigger the browser download path; native Save uses expo-media-library with contextual permission + Settings fallback. The off-screen capture node lives inside the Modal layer at left:-99999/top:-99999 with pointerEvents:'none' so there is no ScrollView layout leak under the modal.
rca_of_issue: No bugs reproduced this iteration. Spot-checked the previous ScrollView layout-leak fix is still intact and that switching between the QA share modal (Scripture qa) and the new Prayer share modal works without state contamination — both unmount on close and re-open fresh.
